rationale: USDJPY is hovering at 40-year highs near 162, a level ING and other banks explicitly cite as the MOF's intervention trigger; Finance Minister Katayama issued fresh verbal warnings on July 2 and the MoF has already deployed a record ¥11.73T in ambush-style FX intervention. With July 4 US holiday thinning liquidity and payroll data as a near-term catalyst, the asymmetric risk over the next 5–10 days skews toward a sharp yen-strengthening episode rather than further meaningful dollar upside, even though the structural carry trade remains intact.
key_drivers: Finance Minister Katayama verbal warnings July 2
Thin holiday liquidity amplifying intervention impact
US payrolls (July 3) downside risk to dollar
BOJ at 1% but skepticism over acceleration caps yen upside
Pair at 91% of 52-week range with asymmetric risk/reward
